frameworks.name: attractor field model
frameworks.one_line: perceived similarity warps perception space so dense regions inflate

frameworks.name: perception-driven mimicry coevolution
frameworks.one_line: predator perception biases drive prey-prey similarity dynamics

frameworks.name: categorical perception of intermediate forms
frameworks.one_line: intermediate morphs are assigned to the rarer category

core_question: How does predator perception shape the coevolutionary dynamics of mimicry between similar prey species?
key_assumptions: morphologically intermediate forms are perceived as belonging to rarer rather than abundant forms
mimicry requires at least two similar organisms and a selective perceiving agent
confusion probability between species exerts pressure on the predator's perception space
manipulations: relative density of species representations in perception space
degree of morphological similarity between model and mimic
key_findings: numerical simulations show perception-space inflation in regions with high species-representation density
inflation increases discriminability between similar species by the predator
atypical (rare) species need less similarity to the model to gain mimicry benefit
interpretations: adaptive mimicry can emerge more easily among atypical species due to perception-space deformation
predator perception dynamics constitute an interface linking prey-prey interactions with sensory cognition
